data.title: Daktronics, Inc. (DAKT) shares dropped after Q3 FY26 results: Adjusted EPS at 9 cents (vs 13-cent estimate), sales of $181.87 million (vs $181 million estimate); Sales rose 21.6% Y/Y, adjusted operating income increased to $4 million.
data.description: Shares of Daktronics, Inc. (NASDAQ: DAKT) dipped after the company released its third-quarter FY26 results. The report showed mixed performance, with an adjusted EPS of nine cents falling short of the estimated 13 cents, while sales reached $181.87 million, surpassing the forecasted $181 million. Sales saw a 21.6% increase year over year, attributed to efficient backlog fulfillment at the start of the quarter. Adjusted operating income also saw a significant rise to $4 million from $1.2 million compared to the same period last year. The company anticipates this trend to continue into the final quarter of fiscal 2026. These details and more are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Wall Street rises on Iran diplomacy hopes. Nasdaq up 1.5% to 25,100, S&P 500 up 0.8% to 6,875. Dow underperforms, down 0.1% to 48,860. Bitcoin surges to $71,000, up 7%. Energy sector lags, XLE down 1.2%. Technology leads, XLK up 1.8%.
data.description: Wall Street experienced a relief rally with reports of potential U.S.-Iran diplomatic talks, mainly driven by a New York Times article hinting at diplomatic dialogue. By midday trading in New York, the Nasdaq 100 gained 1.5% to 25,100, the S&P 500 rose by 0.8% to 6,875, and the Russell 2000 added 1.0% to 2,630. However, the Dow Jones Industrial Average slipped 0.1% to 48,860, influenced by consumer staples weakness. The CBOE Volatility Index dropped 11.6% to 20.84. Technology sectors led the gains, with the Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund up 1.8%. Other sectors like consumer discretionary and communications also saw gains. Energy was the session's laggard with the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund down 1.2%. Bitcoin surged, reaching $71,000 for the first time in a month, rising by 7% and boosting other cryptocurrencies following the trend of de-escalation. Oil prices fell after two consecutive sessions of rallying, and the technology sector advanced as ceasefire odds rose in prediction markets. (character count: 980)

data.title: SiriusXM discloses pricing terms for cash tender offer for all outstanding 3.125% senior notes due 2026. #NASDAQ #SIRI #finance
data.description: On March 4, 2026, Sirius XM Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: SIRI) revealed details regarding a cash tender offer made by its subsidiary, Sirius XM Radio LLC, to buy back all outstanding shares. This offer allows shareholders to sell their shares at a set price determined by the company. The specifics of the pricing terms were disclosed, providing interested parties with information on the financial aspects of the transaction. This tender offer presents an opportunity for investors to liquidate their holdings in Sirius XM Holdings Inc. at a predetermined value.

data.title: The Surrogacy Foundation celebrates first baby born through $100,000 grant program, correcting previous news release.
data.description: The Surrogacy Foundation recently announced the birth of the first baby through its $100,000 grant program. The news was shared on March 4, 2026, by The Surrogacy Foundation via PR Newswire. A representative of the company highlighted that modifications have been implemented.

data.title: Neptune Flood Research analyzes impact of FEMA's proposed 2026 Harris County, TX, flood map update. 70% of flooded homes were outside high-risk zone.
data.description: In Harris County, Texas, FEMA's flood maps had remained stagnant for almost two decades, failing to accurately depict homeowners' flood risk. Hurricane Harvey exposed this flaw as about 70% of flooded homes were located outside the designated high-risk area. The latest draft maps unveiled in February 2026 signify progress towards better aligning public policy with the true hydrological risk in the region. This proposed update aims to address the discrepancy between current mapping and the actual flood risk faced by residents.

data.title: China tech and emerging markets stocks are deeply oversold, potential rebound candidates. KraneShares CSI China Internet ETF (KWEB) tops list with RSI of 18.32, BABA stock follows with RSI of 22.48. JD.com Inc. (JD) shows RSI of 24.42. Market caps of at least $2 billion and avg. 14-day trading volumes exceeding 2 million shares were considered during the search on March 4, 2026. Check Benzinga.com for full story.
data.description: Global equities recently experienced volatility, leading to oversold conditions for certain popular stocks and funds, hinting at potential rebound opportunities if market sentiment improves. A screening on March 4, 2026, focused on stocks with market caps over $2 billion, averaging 14-day volume exceeding 2 million shares, and low relative strength index (RSI) readings. The list included China-focused investments and growth stocks, with the KraneShares CSI China Internet ETF (KWEB) notably at an RSI of 18.32, portraying extreme oversold conditions. Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. (BABA) and JD.com Inc. (JD) also displayed low RSIs (22.48 and 24.42, respectively), with their stock prices around $133.81 and $25.21, witnessing declines amidst concerns over China's growth outlook and regulatory challenges. The article discusses the stocks' performance, indicating potential for rebound amid extreme market conditions.

data.title: Home Helpers Home Care achieves record growth with 41 franchise deals, aiming for 400 territories by 2025, under CEO Emma Dickison. Franchise opportunities available nationwide.
data.description: Renowned CEO and President Emma Dickison is propelling the brand towards a significant milestone of 400 territories. Home Helpers® Home Care, a top player in nonmedical in-home companionship and personal care services across the nation, is offering franchise opportunities nationwide. The strides made under Emma Dickison's leadership position the company for continued success as they expand their reach to more territories.

data.title: Create Music Group raises $450M, valued at $2.2B; secures new equity and debt capital for expansion.
data.description: Create Music Group just concluded its latest funding round in Los Angeles on March 4, 2026. The company's value now stands at $2.2 billion following the successful acquisition of more than $450 million in fresh equity and debt capital. This investment will facilitate the company's ongoing growth and development.

data.title: Nicole Lamorte appointed as Branch Manager in Crystal Lake, IL by Nations Lending Corporation.
data.description: Nations Lending Corporation, a prominent national mortgage lender, has appointed Nicole Lamorte as the Branch Manager for its Crystal Lake, Illinois branch. In this role, Nicole will manage operations, guide originators and staff, and supervise client interactions in McHenry County and nearby areas. Tim Dowling, the SVP Eastern Divisional Manager, expressed enthusiasm about Nicole joining the leadership team in the Chicago region. This move signifies the company's commitment to growth and quality service in the local market.

data.title: Millions worth of ALP Drifters nicotine pouches stolen, prompting national manhunt by Tucker Carlson's ALP.
data.description: In Miami on March 4, 2026, a limited-edition nicotine pouch line called ALP Drifters by ALP Supply Co. was stolen in transit shortly after its public announcement. The stolen shipment, worth millions of dollars, contained a significant number of the newly released product.

data.title: Banking groups criticize Kraken's Fed approval as risky, improper. Kraken is the first crypto bank with a Fed master account.
data.description: On Wednesday, Kraken made history by becoming the first cryptocurrency bank to secure a Federal Reserve master account. This accomplishment has stirred controversy within the banking sector.
